name: Vampire Squid
description: Neither squid nor octopus, this ancient cephalopod cloaks itself in a cape of webbed arms and produces blue bioluminescent light from photophores across its body.

name: Anglerfish
description: The female lures prey with a glowing bioluminescent appendage dangling from her head. The tiny parasitic male fuses permanently to her body, sharing her bloodstream.

name: Dumbo Octopus
description: Named for its ear-like fins that resemble the Disney elephant, this ghostly octopus hovers above the seafloor flapping its fins, swallowing prey whole.

name: Giant Isopod
description: A deep-sea relative of the common pill bug, this armored giant can survive years without food. When it does eat, it gorges itself until it can barely move.

name: Siphonophore
description: One of the longest animals on Earth, this colonial organism is not a single creature but thousands of specialized individuals called zooids acting as one organism.

name: Barreleye Fish
description: Its transparent fluid-filled dome houses rotating tubular eyes that can look upward to spot prey silhouettes or forward when feeding. The green orbs are its eyes; its face is below.

name: Frilled Shark
description: A living fossil largely unchanged for 80 million years. Its 300 trident-shaped teeth and serpentine body make it look more like a sea monster than a shark.

name: Sea Pig
description: A translucent sea cucumber that walks along the ocean floor on water-filled tube feet. They often gather in herds of hundreds, all facing the same current direction.

name: Firefly Squid
description: Each tiny squid carries thousands of photophores that flash in complex blue patterns for communication and counterillumination. Each spring millions surface to spawn in Toyama Bay, Japan.

name: Goblin Shark
description: Its distinctive elongated snout is packed with electroreceptors. When prey is detected, its jaw launches forward in a slingshot motion — one of the fastest jaw strikes of any shark.

name: Ping-Pong Tree Sponge
description: This carnivorous sponge looks like a candelabra hung with Christmas ornaments. The spherical balls are covered in tiny hooks that snare passing crustaceans whole.

name: Stoplight Loosejaw
description: The only fish known to produce and see red bioluminescent light, giving it a secret searchlight invisible to prey. Its jawbones are unconnected to its skull, opening impossibly wide.
